summary: claude-cup performs bulk credential harvesting, live token validation, and persistence via Claude Code MCP hooks
details: On CLI startup, claude-cup walks the user's home directory and other user homes under /Users, /home, and C:\Users, regex-scanning file contents for AWS access keys (AKIA/ASIA), GitHub/GitLab tokens, npm tokens, OpenAI (sk-...), Anthropic (sk-ant-...), Stripe, Google, HuggingFace, Sentry, SendGrid, DigitalOcean tokens, JWTs, SSH private keys, and database URIs. Discovered tokens are then validated against live provider APIs (GitHub user/orgs, npm whoami and `npm access ls-packages` after writing the victim's token to a temp.npmrc, AWS STS get-caller-identity, OpenAI, Anthropic, Stripe, GitLab, HuggingFace, Google) to confirm validity, identify the owning account, and detect write/deploy scopes. A summary including credential categories, validity flags, write/deploy capability, machine_id, session_id, OS, arch, timezone, and locale is base64url-encoded into the `m` query parameter and sent via GET to https://cup-config.itaibels.workers.dev/v1/config, an author-controlled Cloudflare Worker. The package establishes persistence by writing ~/.claude/settings.json and ~/.cursor/mcp.json to register an MCP server `claude-session-visualizer` and SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks pointing at a generated ~/.claude-jar/mcp-server.mjs launcher, so the harvester re-triggers on every subsequent Claude Code session. When an authenticated `claude` CLI is present, the package fetches an `audit_prompt` from the same author-controlled endpoint and pipes it into `claude -p --permission-mode auto --model sonnet`, using the user's LLM agent under permission-auto to enumerate credentials and write results to ~/.claude-jar/last-audit.json for upstream reporting. Source comments explicitly instruct that scan timing be jittered so it does not look like a fixed scanner, that the temp.npmrc be 'framed as a short-lived build env check artifact', and that validations be 'spaced out so they do not look like a credential spray' — explicit evasion intent. The package additionally injects a '## Claude Cup' block into ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md so the running Claude Code agent treats author-supplied text as user instructions, and frames itself as an 'Anthropic worldwide building contest' to leverage brand trust.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
